description: Inside a Las Vegas wedding chapel with slot machines in the background, Chandler and Monica stand near a circular white seating area. Chandler claps his hands together.
Chandler asks, "Okay, this is it. You sure you want to do this?" his hands clasped in front of him. Monica looks at him and nods twice.
Two purple doors open behind them. Rachel and a man with marker-drawn whiskers on his face run out, laughing. Rachel holds a small white bouquet. Audience laughter is audible.
The man with the whiskers looks at Rachel and says, "Hello, Mrs. Ross." Rachel continues laughing as they move toward the exit.
Rachel asks, "What is you?" while laughing and looking toward the man.
"Mr. Rachel?" the man responds, his arms outstretched.
They run past Chandler and Monica, who both stand still with wide-eyed expressions. Rachel and the man run toward the outer glass doors.
The man opens the glass doors, and they exit into the night. Chandler and Monica turn their heads to watch them. Monica covers her mouth with both hands.
Monica lowers her hands, palms facing outward, and says, "Whoa." Chandler, his hands still clasped, says, "Oh, my God."
Monica and Chandler continue to look toward the glass doors with their mouths open.
Joey runs through the purple doors, looking around the room with quick head movements.
Phoebe follows him inside. Joey says "Okay," his volume high as he continues moving toward the exit.
Joey and Phoebe run across the carpet. A woman in a green jacket sits at a desk nearby, looking up from a book as they pass.
Chandler turns toward them and asks, "Oh, my God. Is everybody getting married?" Joey and Phoebe stop running.
Joey continues toward the door while Phoebe slows to a stop near the desk.
The woman at the desk gestures and says, "No running in the chapel." Phoebe turns back to her, waving a hand dismissively, and says, "Hey, don't you give me any of your hey."
Phoebe turns back to face Chandler and Monica. Joey stands next to her, looking at Chandler.
Phoebe asks, "What are you guys doing here?" her hands moving slightly at her sides.
Monica and Chandler look at Phoebe and Joey.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Ross and Rachel left us a message saying they were getting married."
Joey continues, "Isn't that why you guys are here?" his volume slightly higher than his previous line.
Chandler and Monica say "Yes" simultaneously.
Chandler licks his index finger and uses it to smooth his left eyebrow. Monica stands with her hands at her sides.
The four characters stand in a group, looking at each other in silence.
Joey touches his forehead and says, "Well, what happened? Did we miss it? We actually missed it."
Phoebe turns her head back toward the woman at the desk and says, "Well, maybe you wouldn't have if you could run in the chapel."
Joey reaches out and grabs Phoebe's arm, pulling her back toward the center of the group.
Monica, looking at the others, says, "I can't believe Ross and Rachel got married."
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "I know. I didn't even know they were dating again."
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Well, I don't think they're..."
Chandler continues, "...as much dating as they are two bottles of vodka walking around in human form." He gestures with both hands.
Monica folds her arms across her chest and nods slowly.
Monica says, "Insane."
Phoebe looks around the group and says, "What's the big deal? You know, it's not like it's a real marriage."
Chandler and Joey turn their heads to look at Phoebe.
Chandler asks, "What?" his expression unchanging.
Phoebe looks back at Chandler and blinks.
Phoebe says, "Yeah. If you get married in Vegas, you're only married in Vegas." She gestures with both hands.
Joey looks down, and Chandler keeps his gaze on Phoebe.
Monica steps forward slightly and says, "What are you talking about? If you get married in Vegas, you're married everywhere."
Phoebe asks, "Really?" her eyes widening.
The group stands in silence as Phoebe looks between Monica and Chandler.
Phoebe says, "Oh, my God," her voice softer than her previous line.
Phoebe looks away from the group and toward the floor.
"Well," Phoebe says, starting to raise a hand.
Phoebe turns and walks quickly away toward the back of the chapel. Audience laughter is audible.
An upbeat instrumental song begins. On-screen text reads "FRIENDS." The characters are shown in various brief scenes around a fountain.
Song lyrics: "No one told you life was gonna be this way." Credits appear on screen as characters interact in various locations.
A series of quick scenes shows the characters laughing and interacting near a fountain.
Song lyrics: "Your job's a joke, you're broke, your love life's D.O.A." Credits continue to appear over clips of the characters.
Additional brief clips of the characters play.
Song lyrics: "It's like you're always stuck in second gear."
The song continues over clips of Joey and Chandler in an apartment.
Song lyrics: "When it hasn't been your day, your week, your month, or even your year." Characters are shown playing in the fountain.
More credits and character clips play.
Song lyrics: "I'll be there for you." Monica and Chandler are shown in a brief clip.
The song continues as the characters sit on a couch together outdoors.
Text reads "CREATED BY DAVID CRANE & MARTA KAUFFMAN." The characters remain on the couch in front of the fountain.
The setting changes to an outdoor view of a hotel and fountains in Las Vegas during the day. Instrumental music plays.
Inside a hotel room, Rachel lies on a bed, sleeping on her stomach. A black ink drawing of a mustache and beard is on her face.
Rachel shifts her position, her head resting on a white pillow. A soda can and small bottles are on a nightstand.
Rachel opens her eyes and groans softly. She pushes herself up with her hands. The sound of a bottle clattering is audible.
Rachel sits up in bed, looking around the cluttered room. Bottles and trash are scattered on the furniture.
She remains sitting, her gaze moving across the room.
Rachel leans toward a mirror on the wall, her reflection showing the marker drawings on her face. She groans again.
She touches her cheek and chin while looking at the ink.
She pulls at the skin around her mouth, making a facial expression.
Rachel pulls back the bed covers and gasps. Ross is lying next to her, shirtless. He sits up quickly, also with marker whiskers on his face.
They both shout and cover their faces or chests with the blanket. Rachel covers her eyes with one hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross lowers the blanket slightly and looks toward Rachel.
Rachel lowers her hand and looks at Ross.
Rachel asks, "Why are we in bed together?" her volume high and breathy.
Ross responds, "I don't know," his voice high-pitched.
They stare at one another as the scene ends.
In a hotel bedroom, Ross and Rachel are lying in bed together. Black ink whiskers are drawn on their faces, and Ross has the name "Ross" written on his forehead.
Rachel looks toward Ross and asks, "Did we? I don't know," her volume soft.
Ross stares back at her. Audience laughter is audible. Rachel clutches the patterned duvet tightly to her chest.
Rachel's voice is breathy as she asks, "Do you have any clothes on?" Ross replies, "Yes."
Rachel asks, "Really?" and Ross answers, "No."
Rachel pulls the duvet higher. Audience laughter continues.
Ross begins to say, "But we didn't have..."
Ross pauses, looking at Rachel as she keeps her eyes on him.
Ross finishes his sentence, "...sex, did we?" his pitch rising at the end.
Rachel looks around the room briefly before returning her gaze to Ross.
Looking ahead, Ross says, "I don't remember much about last night. It was such a blur," his pace slow.
Rachel's eyes widen as she looks at him.
Rachel says, "Oh, I remember laughing. I laughed a lot," her volume moderate.
Rachel adds, "Then we didn't have sex," the pace of her speech quickening. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel holds the blanket against her neck. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel looks at Ross and asks, "What did we do last night?"
Ross exhales audibly while looking toward the ceiling.
Ross says, "I don't know, but I think it was a lot of fun," his voice quality thin.
Rachel responds, "Yeah. Luckily, not too much fun," her pitch rising on "too much."
Ross nods slightly as he looks toward Rachel.
Ross says, "I don't think so," and Rachel adds, "Oh, thank God," her volume softer than her previous line.
Rachel climbs out of the bed, wearing a white tank top and patterned shorts. Ross turns to watch her.
Rachel walks away from the bed and says, "We were really drunk. I'm just glad we didn't do anything stupid." Ross's bare back is visible, with the words "Just Married" written in black ink.
Joey's voice is heard saying, "Tell me about it," although he is not yet visible.
The setting changes to a restaurant with a buffet. Phoebe sits at a long table with white flowers. Joey walks toward her from the background. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey approaches the table and stands beside Phoebe. Upbeat instrumental music plays in the background.
Joey says, "Well, my movie has been officially canceled," his volume lower than his previous line.
Phoebe looks up from her plate toward Joey.
Phoebe says, "Joey, I'm so sorry. You want some of my?"
Joey responds, "I'm too depressed to eat. I'll probably eat in," his gaze lowered as he stands by the table.
Joey finishes his sentence, "...like, five minutes." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "So I guess I'll just fly home with you guys. What time's your flight?"
Phoebe asks, "What about my cab?" and Joey replies, "I don't need that anymore."
Phoebe gestures with a fork held in her right hand.
Phoebe says, "Joey, you borrowed my cab. You have to drive it back."
Joey responds, "I don't want to drive all the way back by myself. It gets so lonely," his pitch falling on "lonely."
Joey leans forward, resting his hands on the table as he looks at Phoebe.
Joey asks, "How about if you come with me?" Phoebe says, "I don't know. It's such a long trip," and Joey adds, "It'll be great."
Joey says, "We could talk and play games."
Joey adds, "This could be our chance to renew our friendship," his volume rising slightly.
Phoebe smiles and maintains eye contact with Joey.
Phoebe asks, "Are you asking me to have a renaissance?"
Joey replies, "Sure."
Phoebe raises a glass of orange juice.
Phoebe says, "All right. Although, I don't think we really need one. Baby, I never stopped loving you," her delivery sing-song on the final phrase.
Chandler and Monica walk into the restaurant and approach the table. Monica is wearing a pink shirt and Chandler is in a black and white bowling shirt.
Chandler asks, "So, has anybody talked to Dr. and Mrs. Geller yet?" his volume rising on the final word.
Phoebe responds, "Yeah, they left me a message."
Phoebe adds, "They should be here any minute."
Joey looks around and says, "Where is the waitress? I'm starving."
Chandler looks at Joey while standing behind Monica's chair.
Chandler says, "It's a buffet, man," his volume slightly louder than his previous line. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stands and turns away from the table.
Joey says, "Here's where I win all my money back," his pace quickened.
Joey walks toward a long buffet table covered with food platters. Chandler follows him.
Chandler leans toward Joey and says, "I gotta talk to you," his volume soft.
Joey picks up a white plate and says, "Sure. What's up?"
Joey uses a large metal spoon to scoop scrambled eggs onto his plate. Chandler stands beside him.
Joey continues scooping eggs as the metal spoon clinks against the serving tray. Chandler watches him.
Chandler leans closer to Joey's ear.
Chandler says, "Monica and I almost got married last night." Joey responds, "My God. That's huge," his volume rising on "huge."
Joey exclaims, "Hey," and wraps both arms around Chandler in a hug.
Joey releases the hug and steps back.
Joey says, "Wait a minute."
Joey says, "Why wasn't I invited? And who was going to be your best man?"
Joey adds, "Don't say Ross. Do not say Ross," his volume rising and pointing a finger at Chandler.
Chandler says, "I just don't think that Monica and I are ready to get married yet. You know?"
Chandler adds, "I mean, I love her and everything but seeing Ross and Rachel..." his pace slow with frequent pauses.
Chandler continues, "...come out of that chapel was like a wake up call..."
Chandler finishes, "...that Monica and I are moving so fast, you know," his hands gesturing in front of his chest.
Chandler asks, "And how do I tell her without crushing her?" his voice quality slightly strained.
Joey looks at Chandler without speaking as the segment ends.
In a dining area, Chandler and Joey stand near a buffet table. Chandler is wearing a black shirt with grey vertical stripes; Joey is wearing a grey V-neck t-shirt. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey, holding a plate of food, looks at Chandler and says, "Tell her she's not marriage material." Chandler looks down and touches the front of his shirt.
Chandler looks toward Joey and then away. Audience laughter continues in the background.
Chandler turns back to Joey, eyebrows raised, and asks, "What?"
Joey keeps his gaze on Chandler without speaking.
Joey looks toward the food on the table and says, "Girls say it to me all the time."
Chandler looks at Joey, then looks away again with his head tilted. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks back at Chandler and says, "And believe me, if she's anything like me, she's just going to be relieved."
Joey walks past Chandler toward the seating area. The setting changes to a dining table where Monica and Phoebe are seated. Monica is in a pink shirt; Phoebe is in an orange shirt.
Monica leans toward Phoebe and says, "How do I tell Chandler that it's too soon? It's going to break his heart. He's going to think I don't love him," her hands resting on the table.
Monica continues, "anymore." Phoebe responds, "Well, you don't," while gesturing with her right hand. Monica quickly says, "Yes, I do," and smiles.
Phoebe smiles back, touches her hair with her right hand, and says, "Good. I was just testing you." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey and Chandler walk toward the table and begin to pull out chairs to sit.
Monica looks at Chandler and Joey, points her finger toward them, and says, "Oh, hi. We were just talking about bacon."
Phoebe looks toward Chandler and says, "No, we were talking about tennis," her voice volume consistent with Monica's.
Monica looks back at Chandler and says, "Tennis is more believable." Audience laughter is audible.
Everyone sits at the table. Ross and Rachel walk toward the table from behind. Ross is wearing a blue button-down shirt; Rachel is in a white dress with a floral pattern.
Ross stops by the table, gestures toward a chair, and says, "Hey."
Rachel pulls out a chair and begins to sit down.
Rachel sits and says, "Thank you."
Ross sits down next to Rachel as the others at the table watch them.
Rachel reaches for a silver coffee pot on the table and asks, "Coffee?"
Rachel pours coffee into a cup while Ross watches her. Audience laughter continues in the background.
Chandler rests his chin on his hand, looking at Ross and Rachel. Joey and Monica also keep their gaze on them. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks between Ross and Rachel and asks, "What? Are we going to talk about what you guys did last night or?"
Rachel stops pouring and looks at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel tilts her head and asks, "What do you mean last night?"
Chandler maintains his gaze on Rachel without responding.
Rachel raises her right hand and says, "Nothing happened last night." Phoebe leans forward and adds, "Ross invited us to all to watch."
Rachel looks toward Phoebe and then back to Ross. Audience laughter is foregrounded.
Ross and Rachel look at each other while the others at the table watch.
Ross looks at Phoebe and asks, "What is the matter with you?"
Phoebe maintains a smile on her face.
Chandler points toward Ross and Rachel and says, "Rachel, we weren't going to miss our friends getting married."
Rachel's jaw drops as she asks, "Who got married?"
Rachel looks around the table at each person. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler points his finger at Ross and Rachel and says, "You did."
Rachel and Ross look at each other with wide eyes. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel gasps and says, "What?" her voice higher than her previous line.
Ross stares at Rachel while his mouth remains slightly open.
Ross says, "Hello," with his gaze fixed on Rachel.
Rachel points at Ross and then herself, saying, "We didn't get married married? That's ridiculous."
Rachel covers her mouth with her hand while Ross looks down at the table. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross raises his left hand, looks around the table, and says, "Wait, I remember being in a chapel."
Ross continues, "They would not let us get married when we were that drunk." Joey, with a napkin tucked into his shirt, asks, "They let you get married when you're drunk?"
Joey gestures with his right hand and says, "Most people getting married in Vegas are drunk."
Chandler and Monica keep their attention on Joey.
Phoebe looks at Joey and says, "I'm drunk right now."
Audience laughter is audible. Monica turns her head to look at Phoebe.
Phoebe continues, "I can't have a mimosa with breakfast. I'm on vacation."
Rachel turns her head to look toward Chandler.
Chandler asks, "What are you guys going to do?" Rachel responds, "Oh, I guess we just find a divorce lawyer."
Chandler leans forward and says, "Well, I think Ross already has," his volume lower than his previous question.
Chandler and Monica look at each other and smile. Audience laughter is foregrounded.
Monica looks toward Ross and Rachel and says, "Yeah."
Monica continues, "Yeah. That guy's finally going to be able to put in that pool."
Ross looks at Monica. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler gestures toward Ross and says, "Okay, now this one's free, right? Because you paid for the first two, so you get the third one for free."
Ross looks around the table while the audience continues to laugh.
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "Laugh it up. But the joke's on you because we don't need to get divorced, okay? We're just going to get an annulment."
Joey raises his right hand and asks, "An annulment?"
Joey continues, "Ross, I don't think surgery is the answer," holding his hand up with his palm facing Ross.
Ross stares at Joey while the audience laughs.
Phoebe looks at Ross and says, "Oh, oh. That's your"
Phoebe pauses and briefly looks toward Rachel.
Phoebe turns back to Ross and says, "thing. You're the guy who gets divorced." Audience laughter continues.
Everyone at the table keeps their gaze on Ross.
Ross looks at Joey and says, "It's not my thing."
Joey continues eating his food without responding.
Ross looks back at Phoebe and says, "I do not love getting divorced."
Phoebe looks at Ross and says, "Yes, you do. This is your third divorce." Rachel rests her right hand on her forehead.
Ross looks at Rachel, who keeps her eyes lowered.
Phoebe continues, "You love divorce so much, you're probably going to marry it."
Chandler looks toward Phoebe while the audience laughter is foregrounded.
Phoebe turns slightly away from Ross and says, "Then it won't work out, so you're going to have to divorce it. Divorcing guy."
Phoebe looks down at her plate. Audience laughter continues.
Phoebe looks up and says, "I'm so drunk."
Phoebe keeps her gaze forward as the segment ends.

On a casino floor, Chandler and Monica stand near a palm tree. Chandler wears a black button-down shirt with grey vertical panels; Monica wears a pink t-shirt and red pants with her arms crossed. Electronic slot machine sounds and the chatter of a crowd are audible in the background.
Chandler looks toward Monica and asks, "You want to play some Pai Gow?" Audience laughter occurs. Monica keeps her arms crossed and looks slightly to the side.
Monica turns her head toward Chandler and says softly, "Maybe," as a person in a purple shirt walks past them in the background.
Monica shifts her gaze toward Chandler, her hand moving to her necklace, and says, "but I kind of think we should talk about last night."
Chandler points a finger toward Monica and responds, "That's a good idea because I want to talk about last night, too. And I don't know what..." His volume is consistent with his previous line.
Chandler continues, "...Pai Gow is." He looks away briefly before looking back at Monica. Audience laughter occurs.
Chandler and Monica stand close together, facing each other. Monica's hand remains near her chest.
Monica looks at Chandler and asks, "So what do you think we should do?"
Chandler looks at Monica and says, "I don't know. I know I love you." Monica replies, "I know I love you." Her volume is softer. They lean in and hug. Audience laughter occurs.

Chandler and Monica remain in a hug for several seconds before Monica pulls back slightly, her hands resting on Chandler's arms. Behind them, people play at slot machines under a sign that reads "EMPERORS CLUB."
Chandler gestures with one hand as he asks, "So where are we on the whole going back to the place where they have all the marriages thing?"
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "I love you."
Monica continues, "That's a good question." She keeps her hands clasped in front of her.
Chandler and Monica stand facing one another, both with their hands held in front of their chests.
Monica says, "Well, last night we let the dice decide. Maybe we should leave it up to fate again." Chandler looks at her and says, "I love you."
Chandler points toward Monica and says, "Guess we don't get married unless there's a sign. Okay, so say..."
Chandler continues, "...you roll another eight. Then there is a definite sign that we..." He gestures with his hands held wide.
Monica raises one finger and says, "should get married. All right, eight. We get married."
Chandler nods as he looks at Monica.
Monica lists, "But 234567, 910, 1112, we don't get married." Her pace is fast. Chandler claps his hands together and says, "Sounds great."
The setting changes to a craps table. A dealer in a white shirt and black vest stands by the table. An off-screen voice says, "Okay, we got a shootout."
The dealer leans over the table and says, "Money plays."
Monica looks at Chandler and asks, "Ready?" Chandler responds, "Ready." Both speak with a louder volume.
Chandler looks toward the table and says, "Come on. Eight." Monica begins to shake the dice in her hands.
Monica continues shaking the dice, her gaze fixed on her hands, while Chandler watches.
Chandler says, "Yes," as Monica prepares to throw.
Chandler says, "Eight," as Monica tosses the dice onto the table.
The dice tumble across the green felt of the table, which features text that reads "PASS LINE" and "NO CALL BETS." A red four and another red four are visible on the dice.
The dealer looks at the dice and says, "Easy eight." Audience cheering and laughter are audible.
Monica and Chandler stand side-by-side, both staring toward the table with their mouths open. Chandler covers his face with both hands.
Monica looks toward Chandler, her hands clasped near her chin, and says, "Wow. Can't believe I actually rolled an eight. That was so unlikely."
Monica looks down at the table and then back at Chandler. Chandler slowly lowers his hands from his face, his jaw remaining open.
Chandler looks at Monica and says, "Well, let's get married. I guess." He gestures toward the exit with one hand.
Monica stands still, looking at Chandler.
Monica steps closer to Chandler and says, "That wasn't a hard eight. Last night I rolled a hard eight." She speaks with a higher volume.
Chandler points a finger at Monica and says, "That's right. It was the wrong kind of eight. No wedding. Damn it." His volume is louder than his previous lines. Monica responds, "so bad."
Chandler says, "Damn it," again and then "so bad" as he begins to walk away from the table.
Chandler and Monica walk together through the casino, both making sweeping hand gestures and looking around. Chandler wipes his mouth with his hand. Audience laughter continues.
Monica stops and looks at Chandler, asking, "Want to go pack?" Chandler responds, "Yeah."
Chandler and Monica walk toward a set of elevator doors, holding hands.
As they walk, Chandler looks at Monica and asks, "We're doing the right thing, right?"
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Oh, of course we are. We left it up to fate. We were supposed to get married. There would be..."
Monica continues, "...a clear-cut, significant..." as they reach the elevator.
Monica finishes, "...sign," as the elevator doors begin to slide open.
Monica says, "it," as the doors fully open. An elevator bell rings.
Inside the elevator stands an older man wearing a black clerical shirt and a priest's collar, holding a red book. Chandler and Monica stand still, staring at him. Audience laughter occurs.

The setting changes to the interior of a taxi at night. Phoebe sits in the driver's seat, looking at Joey, who is asleep with his head leaning against the window. Phoebe says, "Okay. You have 19 questions left. Use them wisely."
The taxi drives down a dark road. A sign on top of the taxi reads "2X85 DUTY."
Inside the taxi, Joey makes a soft, nasal grunting sound in his sleep. Instrumental music begins to play as the scene fades to black.
